chapter_title.en: Chapter 64 – Guardianship of Al Saibah (Unconditionally freed slave)

text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ مَمْلُوكٍ أُعْتِقَ سَائِبَةً قَالَ يَتَوَلَّى مَنْ شَاءَ وَ عَلَى مَنْ تَوَلَّاهُ جَرِيرَتُهُ وَ لَهُ مِيرَاثُهُ قُلْتُ فَإِنْ سَكَتَ حَتَّى يَمُوتَ قَالَ يُجْعَلُ مَالُهُ فِي بَيْتِ مَالِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Isa Bin Ubeyd, from Yunus, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Suleyman Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a slave who emancipated a slave unconditionally. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘His guardian would be whoever he so like, and upon the one who is his guardian would be his crimes, and for him would be his inheritance’. I said, ‘So if it remains such until he dies (without a guardian)?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘His wealth would be made to be in the public treasury of the Muslims’.
